title: Roast Duck Breast with Pedro Ximinez Cherries
summary: This recipe for duck breast will see you never reach for the chicken again. If home cooking is your thing but you’ve never thought much of duck, this indulgent recipe is here to change your mind. Duck with fruit is an age-old combination, but we’ve added some Pedro Ximénez sherry for a deeply fruity, boozy sauce, packed full of cherries.

method.description: Use a sharp knife to score the skin of each duck breast in shallow cuts in a diamond pattern. Season really well with salt all over.
Cut the cherries in half and remove the stones. Halve, peel and finely chop the shallot.

Put the duck breasts skin-side down into a cold frying pan, then put over a medium-high heat. Once the pan is hot and the duck breasts are really sizzling, cook for 5 mins, having a peek at the skin – it should be super golden and crisp and the fat should have rendered into the pan.

Put a wire rack over a baking tray and put the duck breasts onto it, skin-side up. Put into the oven for 8-10 mins (depending on size) to roast. After this, move onto a plate, still skin-side up, and leave to rest for 5 mins.

Meanwhile, put the duck fat pan over a low heat and scrape in the shallots along with a pinch of salt. Cook for 5 minutes until the shallot is soft. Pour in the chicken stock and sherry, turn the heat to medium and bring to a bubble. Scatter in the cherries and spoon in the cherry jam. Bubble for 8-10 minutes until the cherries are just softening and the sauce is glossy and thickened slightly. Season lightly with salt and plenty of cracked black pepper.
Slice the duck breasts and pour any resting juices into the sauce and serve.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
